description: Salesforce Case, Account, Contact to ServiceNow Incident, Account, User. ITSM integration.
criticalRules: ServiceNow REST API uses Basic Auth or OAuth — never hardcode credentials
ServiceNow sys_id is the internal record ID — store as external ID in Salesforce for update
ServiceNow accepts dates as yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss format (no T, no Z)
ServiceNow reference fields need sys_id of the referenced record, not display value
Priority mapping: Salesforce priority string to ServiceNow integer (1=Critical, 2=High, 3=Moderate, 4=Low)
ServiceNow table API: POST to /api/now/table/{tableName}, PATCH with sys_id to update
Empty string is valid in ServiceNow — use null to clear a field

keyMappings.state: Case.Status: New→1, In Progress→2, Resolved→6, Closed→7

dateConversion: fun toSNDate(d) = if (d != null and d != '') ((d as DateTime) as String {format: 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'}) else null
commonFailures: Priority type error: ServiceNow expects integer, Salesforce sends string
Reference field wrong: sending display value instead of sys_id
Date format rejected: ISO 8601 with T and Z not accepted — use yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
Duplicate incidents: correlation_id not set — always set for idempotency
